subject: Sociology
title: Food Security and Hunger
description: A short lesson to help you apply Food Security and Hunger.

hook.text: The world produces enough food for 10 billion people. Yet 800 million go hungry. This is a distribution problem.

buildup.text: Food insecurity is the lack of reliable access to affordable, nutritious food — a crisis of systems, not supply.

discovery.text: Global agriculture is dominated by multinational corporations that prioritize profit over local food needs.

twist.text: Climate change threatens harvests globally. The poorest farmers face the worst impacts with the fewest resources.

climax.text: Hunger is not caused by scarcity. It's caused by inequality — who grows, who owns, and who eats are all political.

punchline.text: Hunger is a choice — society's, not nature's.

quiz.question: Why does hunger persist despite sufficient global food production?
